_comment: Battle day/phase manifest (ADR 0005; docs/format/battle-manifest.md). A phase is either a battle file (status reconstructed - startTime/endTime MUST echo that file's own values, test-enforced) or an honest not-reconstructed placeholder whose note renders verbatim as the day's empty state. July 3 afternoon is the shipped reconstruction: startTime 46800 (13:00 LMT, ED-24/ED-31 frame).
name: Gettysburg — July 1–3, 1863

phases.label: The first day
phases.note: Not yet reconstructed. The research corpus carries July 1 at dossier grain (passes 10–11: the railroad cut, the Seminary Ridge collapse, the town retreat), but no July 1 tracks have been authored — nothing is shown rather than something invented.

phases.label: The second day
phases.note: Not yet reconstructed. The research corpus carries July 2 at dossier grain (passes 6–9: the Peach Orchard, the Wheatfield waves W1–W5, East Cemetery Hill), but no July 2 tracks have been authored — nothing is shown rather than something invented.

phases.label: Morning — Culp's Hill and the quiet center
phases.note: Not yet reconstructed. The morning fight (Culp's Hill fire to ~10:30–11:00, Taft's 8 A.M. window, Shaler's 9–11 A.M. engagement) sits in the corpus as documented silence on the afternoon file's citations; it becomes this phase's content in a later slice (authoring-wave-a2.md §6.4).

phases.label: Afternoon — the bombardment and the charge
